ddls:
CREATE TABLE "mpi_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "state" TEXT /* State of the process (e.g., State) */,
  "rank" TEXT /* Rank of the process */,
  "mpi_state" TEXT /* State of the MPI function */,
  "timestamp_start" REAL /* Timestamp when the state starts */,
  "timestamp_end" REAL /* Timestamp when the state ends */,
  "duration" REAL /* Duration of the state */,
  "mpi_function" TEXT /* Name of the MPI function being executed */,
  "application_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset version */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment where the performance was measured */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mpi_performance_mpi_function FOREIGN KEY ("mpi_function") REFERENCES mpi_functions ("function_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mpi_performance_rank FOREIGN KEY ("rank") REFERENCES processes ("rank"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mpi_performance_application_id FOREIGN KEY ("application_id") REFERENCES applications ("application_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mpi_performance_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES jobs ("job_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mpi_performance_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mpi_functions" (
  "function_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each MPI function */,
  "function_name" TEXT /* Name of the MPI function */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the MPI function */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category to which the function belongs */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("function_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mpi_functions_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES performance_categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "processes" (
  "process_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each process */,
  "process_name" TEXT /* Name of the process */,
  "rank" TEXT /* Rank of the process */,
  "host_id" INTEGER /* ID of the host where the process is running */,
  "application_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment where the process is running */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("process_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_processes_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES jobs ("job_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_processes_host_id FOREIGN KEY ("host_id") REFERENCES hosts ("host_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_processes_application_id FOREIGN KEY ("application_id") REFERENCES applications ("application_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hosts" (
  "host_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each host */,
  "host_name" TEXT /* Name of the host */,
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* ID of the cluster where the host is located */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the host */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the host */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment where the host is running */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the host (e.g., active, maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("host_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hosts_cluster_id FOREIGN KEY ("cluster_id") REFERENCES clusters ("cluster_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "clusters" (
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cluster */,
  "cluster_name" TEXT /* Name of the cluster */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the cluster */,
  "admin_email" TEXT /* Admin email for the cluster */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment where the cluster is running */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the cluster (e.g., active, maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cluster_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance metric being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the performance metric */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the performance metric was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "device" TEXT /* Device used for access (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("performance_id") REFERENCES mpi_performance ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_files" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance file */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the performance data file */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the performance data file in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the file was uploaded */,
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* ID of the cluster where the file was collected */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset version */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of file (e.g., CSV, JSON) */,
  "checksum" TEXT /* Checksum to ensure data integrity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_files_cluster_id FOREIGN KEY ("cluster_id") REFERENCES clusters ("cluster_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_files_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the performance metric */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the performance metric */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category to which the metric belongs */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES performance_categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance data value */,
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance metric entry */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance metric */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_data_performance_id FOREIGN KEY ("performance_id") REFERENCES mpi_performance ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_data_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the performance category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "application_name" TEXT /* Name of the application */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the application */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the application */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the application (e.g., active, archived) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("application_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "jobs" (
  "job_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job */,
  "job_name" TEXT /* Name of the job */,
  "application_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the job */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the job */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the job (e.g., running, completed, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset version */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the version */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the version */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the version (e.g., active, archived) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the user role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the user role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user role */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)
